name: Sepsis - Elevated Lactate Query
description: Tests ability to find sepsis patients via elevated lactate (>2 mmol/L = septic shock criterion in Sepsis-3 definition). First lactate phenotype. Includes Path D (cryptic shock — elevated lactate without sepsis dx).
prompt: Find patients with elevated serum lactate results greater than 2 mmol/L.

prompts.broad: Find patients with serum lactate measurements above 2 mmol/L — indicating tissue hypoperfusion. Threshold for septic shock per Sepsis-3 definition.
prompts.expert: Search Observation resources with LOINC 2524-7 (Lactate in Serum) where value-quantity > 2 mmol/L. Query: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2524-7&value-quantity=gt2||mmol/L

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated lactate. Includes Path D (cryptic shock).
